title: Drop Basket
desc: The fast-drop supply basket to the lower station is sticking in its guide line again.

steps.text:
The lower posts of Mount Pillar get many things by relay and at least one thing by gravity. A reinforced drop basket runs from Oipont's Lookout to the mid-slope station for wax boards, signal cloth, dry rations, and any object that needs to move faster than a walking messenger can carry it without breaking stride. Today the basket has begun catching on the guide line halfway down, which makes a fast system merely theatrical.

The line hand runs the empty basket a few feet and frowns when it snags. "Free the guide rollers if you want the basket itself," she says. "Or retension the return rope on the upper drum if you prefer the winch. Either one may solve it. I would rather not learn which by dropping supplies experimentally."

options.label: Free the guide rollers on the drop basket
Retension the return rope on the upper drum

steps.text:
The basket rides on paired rollers that hug the guide line and keep the frame from twisting as it descends. A little packed grit in the upper roller housing has turned the whole assembly temperamental. You clear the housing, oil the pins, and spin each roller until it turns with the easy hard motion the line hand expects from equipment this exposed.

The next test run drops cleanly off the platform, sings down the line, and vanishes toward the lower post without the ugly mid-span jerk it had before. The line hand leans over the rail, watching the basket shrink. "Good. If we are going to trust physics, we might as well remove the avoidable objections first."

options.label: Latch the roller case and leave the basket running smooth

steps.text:
The return rope winds through a drum and counterweight so the basket can be hauled back up after a drop without demanding a full squad's resentment. The upper tension has slackened, leaving the line just loose enough to contribute its own little hesitation at the steepest part of the run. You tighten the wrap, reset the drum notch, and test the pull until the return line answers with the firm rising draw the system was designed for.

The line hand sends the basket down again, then draws it back up in one smooth uninterrupted climb. That seems to restore her faith in logistics and possibly the species. "Better," she says. "A supply line that pauses dramatically is only useful to audiences."

options.label: Pin the drum and leave the return line true
